analysis_result: Log-rank test p-value = 1.48e-05 (test statistic = 27.63, df = 4) comparing progression-free survival across all 5 methylation subgroups (MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2, PremalignantNF_G3, Benign_NF-G4, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5). The comparison is highly statistically significant, indicating progression-free survival differs across methylation subgroups.
denominator_used: N = 75 samples with complete data on Methylation_Group, Recurrence, and Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up from the MOESM10 'Fig 1b' sheet. Group sizes: MPNST_G1 n=8 (6 events), MPNST_G2 n=8 (3 events), PremalignantNF_G3 n=29 (9 events), Benign_NF-G4 n=20 (1 event), Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 n=10 (0 events). Total events = 19.
assumptions_made: Recurrence coded 0/1 represents censored/event; Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up is in years; progression-free survival equals time-to-recurrence with death-without-recurrence treated per the source file as censoring (no separate death indicator provided); all 75 samples included (no missing values); multivariate log-rank test used to compare all 5 methylation groups simultaneously (lifelines multivariate_logrank_test); no stratification variables applied.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 1b'
   - Shape: 75 rows x 3 columns after header
   - Columns: Methylation_Group (str), Recurrence (0/1), Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up (years)
   - No missing values in any column

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - All 75 samples from the Fig 1b source data retained
   - Groups (5 methylation subgroups):
     * MPNST_G1: n=8
     * MPNST_G2: n=8
     * PremalignantNF_G3: n=29
     * Benign_NF-G4: n=20
     * Sporadic Spinal NF_G5: n=10

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Time variable: 'Time to Recurrence or Last Follow Up' (years); range 0.00-20.03, median 1.36
   - Event variable: 'Recurrence' (0=censored, 1=recurrence event); 19 events / 56 censored
   - Group variable: 'Methylation_Group' (5 categorical levels)

D. COMPUTATION
   - Method: lifelines.statistics.multivariate_logrank_test across all 5 methylation groups simultaneously
   - Call: multivariate_logrank_test(event_durations=Time, groups=Methylation_Group, event_observed=Recurrence)
   - Degrees of freedom: 4 (k-1 for k=5 groups)

E. RESULT
   - Log-rank chi-square test statistic: 27.6323
   - Degrees of freedom: 4
   - P-value: 1.48e-05 (1.480684e-05)
   - KM median PFS per group: MPNST_G1 = 0.67y; MPNST_G2 = 1.39y; PremalignantNF_G3 = 11.79y; Benign_NF-G4 = not reached; Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 = not reached

F. CAVEATS
   - Event-rate imbalance is strong: MPNSTs (G1/G2) show early recurrences while benign/premalignant/sporadic groups have few or no events (G5 has 0 events), which drives the large chi-square
   - Small per-group sample sizes (particularly G1, G2, G5) limit pairwise interpretability, though the omnibus multi-group log-rank is still valid
   - Time is treated as years as provided; unit not independently verified
   - Assumed proportional hazards only in a loose sense -- log-rank test itself does not require PH but is most powerful when hazards are proportional
